## Background
<!-- 1–3 sentences: what situation, problem, or prior work led to this session.
     Keep it tight — this is context, not a specification. -->

this session-0001 in this current story "migration" shall be the foundation for a well-planned, stetp-by-step-migration to a new repository, which shall eolve over several seperate sessions. as much, as needed.

it is time to move on. i tend to focus on gold-plating in this repo. but the JOSYN-project must go on.\
so i want a clean milestone-finish and an entirely new repo, to work on.\
this repos should be logically sealed and archived.\
in a new repo, there shall be no relevant information loss, but a fresh, cleaned base to proceed to the next milestone.\
the new repo will have one new purpose: collaboration and commuication: the first time, the world (that' my architecture and developer collegues) will be confronted with this first milestone, that until now was a "lonley ride"...\

## Goals (for this session)
<!-- numbered list of concrete goals — what "done" looks like from your perspective.
     Be specific. Each goal should be independently verifiable. -->
1. collaboratively define the scope of the migration:   
    - what should be moved, what should be left behind, and what should be changed during the migration.
    - which partial-goals can be sharply defined
    - vision: which qualities and characteristics should the new repo have - what is its main purpose.
     
2. this first session can be closed, when a good and well documented understanding of this foundation is established, and a first plan for the next steps is outlined.

## Keythoughts

We should always have a dual view on the repo-character:

1. the main-aspcet: the substancial content, what the collegues see, the code, the documentaion of the different builduing-blocks and the overall architecture, the "what" and "how" of the project.\

2. i call it: "my agent layer" 
    - the "story-method" shall survive
    - your agent-perspective must be satisfied, and you should be able to work with the new repo as well, without any loss of information a degration in our collaboration-expeience.\
